description: An act or manner is 'muko' when it receives less attention than was reasonably available for avoiding a foreseeable mistake or harm. A careless cut may miss its mark; a careless message may expose a confidence. 'thesa' (careful) directs attention to such consequences, while 'thesua' (mindful) keeps deliberate attention on the present act more broadly.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for voiced nasal 'm', carry the voice through the nose, and raise the back of the tongue into stressed high rounded 'u'. Close the back of the tongue against the soft palate for unvoiced 'k' and release it into true-mid rounded back 'o'.
sound_symbolism: The lips close for a voiced hum, then the back of the tongue makes a full stop and releases. Two firm closures give this short warning word a clipped contour.
usage_notes: Before an action, 'muko' gives careless manner. A person predicate is a judgement about conduct in context rather than a permanent identity. As a noun, the form means carelessness.

pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: A careless act can be corrected without turning the actor into a fixed kind of person. 'muko' can stay on the conduct being discussed.

semantic_domains.activity: an action performed without attention reasonably available for avoiding mistakes or harm
semantic_domains.cognition: insufficient attention to foreseeable consequences in a stated act
